see_evidence: OWASP 2026 ASI06: Memory poisoning reshaped behaviour long after initial interaction
Dev|Journal drift detection: tracks validator compliance, length drift, semantic similarity, regression
KDnuggets Apr 2026: schema validation + drift scoring + severity-graded reports

validation_tests.test: T1 detect baseline_sha=file_path bug (real current bug caught)
T2 clean valid sync passes all 3 passes
T6 unknown but valid SHA → WARN only, not BLOCK

real_bug_caught.description: Current CLAUDE_MEMORY_SYNC_v1.json has baseline_sha set to a file path instead of a SHA-256 hex digest. The sync_writer resolved CURRENT_WORKING_BASELINE_POINTER which pointed to a receipt file path. The validator blocks this immediately with P2_BASELINE_SHA_IS_PATH.
real_bug_caught.fix_needed_in: claude_memory_sync_writer_v1.py — baseline_sha resolution must validate output is 64-char hex, not a path

new_capabilities: CAPABILITY: Pass 1 — schema compliance: required fields, _sha self-hash integrity, type checks
CAPABILITY: Pass 2 — content integrity: baseline_sha format, path-vs-SHA detection, amendment count bounds
CAPABILITY: Pass 3 — drift detection: amendment regression, baseline change, UTC regression (replay), note truncation
CAPABILITY: Composite drift score 0.0–1.0 with configurable block threshold (0.75)
CAPABILITY: Validation receipt written on every run (PASS or BLOCK)
CAPABILITY: History ledger updated on non-blocked runs for future drift comparison
CAPABILITY: Fail-closed: BLOCK verdict exits 1 and prevents sync from being loaded
next_correct_stage: Fix baseline_sha resolution bug in claude_memory_sync_writer_v1.py
